topic: Claude Code + GitHub Workflow 2026: PR Reviews, Commits, and CI Integration

competitors.title: Build Claude Code PR Reviews with GitHub Actions 2026
key_points: Step-by-step guide to wiring claude-code-action@v1 into GitHub Actions for automated PR reviews
Claude Sonnet 4 costs $3/MTok input and $15/MTok output; a 400-line diff typically runs under $0.05 per review
Use paths filtering at workflow trigger level to skip lock files and auto-generated code, saving 30-50% on token costs
system_prompt is the highest-leverage config option — describe team standards once and every PR inherits them
post_as_review: true submits a formal GitHub PR Review (not just a comment), appearing in the approvals section
review_event can be set to COMMENT, APPROVE, or REQUEST_CHANGES for different review behaviors
For a team of 10 engineers merging 20 PRs/day, cost runs ~$24/month with path filtering
Supported on private repos with identical setup (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret + pull-requests: write permission)

competitors.title: Claude Code in GitHub Actions: A Complete Guide to Automated PR Fixes
key_points: Launched September 29, 2025 as part of Claude Code 2.0, built on Anthropic's Agent SDK
Two modes: Interactive mode (@claude mentions in comments) and Automation mode (prompt parameter in YAML for headless execution)
Four core workflow patterns: Interactive comment trigger, Automated PR code review, CI failure auto-fix, Structured output for downstream decisions
CI failure auto-fix is the highest-impact pattern — Claude creates a fix branch, diagnoses failure, pushes a patch, and opens a PR for human review
Loop prevention guard: if condition excludes Claude's own fix branches from triggering new fix attempts
Cost controls: concurrency groups limit parallel review jobs, --max-turns caps iterations, prompt specificity reduces unnecessary API calls
Supports four auth backends: Anthropic direct API, Claude Code OAuth (Max plan), AWS Bedrock OIDC, Google Vertex AI OIDC
Most teams running 50 PRs/month see under $5/month in API costs

competitors.title: Claude Code GitHub Actions — Official Documentation
key_points: Official Anthropic action: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 — run full Claude Code runtime inside standard GitHub Actions runner
Quick setup: run /install-github-app inside Claude Code terminal to auto-install app, configure secrets, create starter workflow
v1.0 breaking changes: mode auto-detected (no more mode: tag/agent), direct_prompt → prompt, CLI options moved to claude_args
Supports @claude mentions for interactive mode and prompt parameter for automation mode
Skills: invoke installed skills directly from the prompt parameter
Cloud provider support: AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI via OIDC for enterprise data residency without direct Anthropic billing
Key parameters: anthropic_api_key (required), prompt (optional for comment mode), claude_args (CLI passthrough), trigger_phrase (default @claude)
Security: never commit API keys, use GitHub Secrets, limit action permissions to minimum necessary

competitors.title: AI Code Review and Automated Code Quality Tools 2026 — Zylos Research
key_points: 84% of developers now regularly use AI-assisted coding tools; 41% of commits are AI-assisted
AI code review market projected to reach $750M with 9.2% CAGR through 2033
Leading tools detect 42-48% of real-world runtime bugs with 85-95% overall accuracy and 5-15% false positive rates
Review capacity, not development speed, now limits delivery velocity — the primary 2026 challenge
20% of companies currently using AI to review 10-20% of PRs, expected to rise significantly in 2026
CodeRabbit is most widely adopted AI code review platform: 2M+ repos, 13M+ PRs reviewed
Code assistant adoption grew from 49.2% (Jan 2025) to 69% (Oct 2025), peaking at 72.8% in August 2025
Industry evolved from simple line-by-line analysis to sophisticated agent-based cross-repository review

angles: From manual bottleneck to automated gatekeeper: How Claude Code GitHub Actions eliminates the review capacity crisis that 84% of developer teams face
The four workflow patterns every team should know: Interactive comment trigger, automated PR review, CI failure auto-fix, and structured output for downstream decisions
Cost reality check: Under $5/month for 50 PRs vs. the $250K/year savings — why the ROI is undeniable
Security-first CI/CD: How to deploy Claude Code Actions with proper guardrails (loop prevention, permission scoping, CLAUDE.md behavioral constraints)
Enterprise-ready AI code review: AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI integration for data residency without direct Anthropic billing

Pattern 2: Automated PR Code Review — Pre-Triage Every Pull Request
